substrate_condition: clean, uniform dark brown clay/lava-rock style pellet substrate with no visible debris
concerns: Moss clump appears unanchored or loosely resting on substrate — fronds are splaying outward and upward in a somewhat disheveled pattern, which may indicate it has detached or is not tied down
Some stem/branch material visible at the moss base appears dark or blackened, worth monitoring for die-off
narrative: A dense, bright green moss clump — likely java moss or a similar fine-leaved aquatic moss — sits directly on the substrate near the rear corner of the tank, with one vivid red cherry shrimp visible nestled at its base, actively grazing or resting. The moss shows healthy green coloration overall but has some wispy, loose fronds extending outward and a few darker sections at the base that may warrant a closer look.
owner_comment: moss looks pretty healthy and does not appear to be melting
